texto: On the other side of the ledger stood the fact that fotitude was useless against it (liquor). Even the mightiest potsman, a paladin who could match tankards with a whole alehouse of swag-bellies Falstaffs and outquaff the parcel of them, would see his length measured upon the floor by less liquid than it would take to fill his hat.
explicacao: Citação de David Wondrich sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “On the other side of the ledger stood the fact that fotitude was useless against it (liquor). Eve…” articula reflexao, drinking, would em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.
